build_from.reason: This is the complete, authoritative schema/prompt/fixture contract that any new persona — including the model-tuned Claude and GPT-4 variants this epic ships — must satisfy: required agent fields (provider, model), the canonical prompt template structure with required template variables ({{.AgentName}}, {{.ScopeRule}}, etc.), the fixture location/naming convention (personas/testdata/<slug>_fixture.patch), and the contribution checklist. Tasks 1-2 (authored in the external atcr/personas repo) must follow this exact contract; this repo's own docs/personas-install.md and README already assume it.
build_from.suggested_approach: Task 1-2 content (external atcr/personas repo, out of scope for this codebase) follows docs/personas-authoring.md's template + checklist verbatim for each model-tuned persona. Task 3 (the only in-repo work) updates docs/personas-install.md's early sections/Quick walkthrough and README.md's Quickstart to recommend installing the new default persona pack as part of first-time setup, reusing the existing `atcr personas install <name>` / `atcr personas search <keyword>` commands documented there — no new command, flag, or code path is needed.

existing_patterns.name: Community Persona Distribution (Epic 9.0)
existing_patterns.description: `atcr personas install/search/list/upgrade/remove/test` fetches persona YAML (a superset of a registry agent) plus an index.json from a configurable base URL (ATCR_PERSONAS_URL, default the public atcr/personas GitHub repo).
existing_patterns.follow_for: This epic ships content through this existing mechanism only — no new command, schema, or hosting is introduced.

existing_patterns.name: Persona Authoring Contract
existing_patterns.description: A persona YAML binds provider+model (required) plus optional persona/role/language fields; its prompt is a Markdown template with a fixed section structure and required template variables; a fixture proves it works without a live LLM call.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Every new model-tuned persona (Claude-tuned, GPT-4-tuned) authored in the external atcr/personas repo must conform to this contract.

existing_patterns.name: Personas CLI Subcommand Surface
existing_patterns.description: `cmd/atcr/personas.go` registers six sub-subcommands (`install`, `search`, `list`, `upgrade`, `remove`, `test`) backed by `internal/personas/`. These commands are the only in-repo surface affected by this epic's content.
existing_patterns.follow_for: No new subcommand or flag is needed; documentation should reference the existing `atcr personas install/search` commands.

Tasks 1-2 (authoring persona YAML + prompt templates and adding index.json entries) land in the separate `atcr/personas` GitHub repository; this plan cannot verify them directly through its single-repo TDD loop

reusable_components.name: atcr personas install/search
reusable_components.usage_example: atcr personas install <namespace>/<name> — the exact mechanism this epic's new personas are installed through; no extension needed

architecture_notes: This epic performs almost no in-repo code change. The only in-repo component is docs/ (docs/personas-install.md + README.md Quickstart) — everything else (Tasks 1-2: authoring persona YAML/prompt content and publishing index.json entries) lands in the separate atcr/personas GitHub repository.
No new registry.yaml schema, no atcr.dev hosting, and no new CLI surface are introduced — this is purely new content flowing through Epic 9.0's existing distribution mechanism.

Because the substantive work is cross-repo, user stories/ACs generated for this plan should scope narrowly to the in-repo documentation change (Task 3) and describe Tasks 1-2 informationally as external dependencies, not as work this plan's TDD loop can execute.
